pattern: A subarray's sum is odd exactly when its two prefix-sum endpoints have different parity, so tracking running even/odd prefix counts turns the count into a single linear pass.
hints: A subarray's sum is odd exactly when the prefix sums at its two endpoints have different parity.
Keep running counts of how many prefix sums seen so far are even and how many are odd, starting with one even prefix for the empty prefix before the array begins.
For each new running sum, add the opposite-parity count to the answer, then bump that parity's own counter, all taken modulo 1e9 + 7.

edge_inputs.description: all even values produce zero odd subarrays
large-valued elements still governed only by parity
mixed distinct values of both parities

stress.description: moderate random array over the full value range
